title: LaplacianRecursion
summary: Formalizes the iterative ambient-Laplacian recursion corresponding to Proposition 3.7 in the PE renormalized-integral reference.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Formalizes the iterative ambient-Laplacian recursion corresponding to Proposition 3.7 in the PE renormalized-integral reference.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/PE/Invariants/LaplacianRecursion.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: case Laplacian Coefficient. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def caseLaplacianCoefficient (n k j : Nat) : Rat

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Case Laplacian Factor. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def CaseLaplacianFactor (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Case Laplacian Factor weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianFactor_weight (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : (CaseLaplacianFactor n k j I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Case Laplacian Recursion. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def CaseLaplacianRecursion (n k : Nat) : Nat -> ScalarInvariant -> ScalarInvariant | 0, I => I | ell + 1, I => CaseLaplacianFactor n k ell (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Case Laplacian Recursion zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_zero (n k : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : CaseLaplacianRecursion n k 0 I = I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Case Laplacian Recursion succ. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_succ (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : CaseLaplacianRecursion n k (ell + 1) I = CaseLaplacianFactor n k ell (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Case Laplacian Recursion weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_weight (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: ambient Laplacian Iter. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def ambientLaplacianIter : Nat -> AmbientScalarInvariant -> AmbientScalarInvariant | 0, I => I | ell + 1, I => AmbientScalarInvariant.laplacian (ambientLaplacianIter ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: ambient Laplacian Iter zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_zero (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : ambientLaplacianIter 0 I = I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: ambient Laplacian Iter succ. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_succ (ell : Nat) (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : ambientLaplacianIter (ell + 1) I = AmbientScalarInvariant.laplacian (ambientLaplacianIter ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: ambient Laplacian Iter weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_weight (ell : Nat) (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : (ambientLaplacianIter ell I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Einstein Ambient Laplacian Formula. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: straightenable laplacian step. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_laplacian_step [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: straightenable laplacian iterate. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_laplacian_iterate [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: ambient laplacian iter einstein slice conformal. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambient_laplacian_iter_einstein_slice_conformal [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n ell : Nat) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hCritical : Itilde.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int) = -(n : Int)) :

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
